sentence_text_2: If a people's rights are contingent upon the whims of a monarch, they possess no real rights at all
sentence_text_1: The act of consenting to join a community signifies a commitment to abide by the decisions made by the majority

sentence_text_2: Kings were often appointed under conditions that required them to respect the rights and liberties of the people, indicating that their power was not absolute
sentence_text_1: True consent and election require an explicit act of approval from the governed

sentence_text_2: The existence of a republic is indeed dependent on the consent of the governed
sentence_text_1: Nations have consistently exercised the right to challenge unjust rulers

sentence_text_2: Rulership legitimacy does not rely on the consent of the governed
sentence_text_1: Ruler authority does not depend on the consent of the people

sentence_text_2: A king cannot unilaterally impose laws without parliamentary approval
sentence_text_1: The essence of rightful governance is rooted in justice and legitimacy

sentence_text_2: Individuals maintain their liberty while agreeing to be governed by the majority's decisions
sentence_text_1: The people must have the freedom to consent to their rulers, and until such consent is granted, any authority exercised by an usurper is devoid of legitimacy

sentence_text_2: The people or their representatives are the only rightful judges of whether a ruler fulfills the purpose of their authority
sentence_text_1: The power of a king is not derived solely from the law

sentence_text_2: The King acts as a minister or agent for the people
sentence_text_1: When a society faces corruption or tyranny, it is the collective judgment of the people that determines the necessity for change in governance

sentence_text_2: The need for accountability and transparency in governance is paramount
sentence_text_1: Disregarding the well-being of subjects can lead to oppression and resistance

sentence_text_2: When a king acts outside the law, he undermines the very foundation of his authority and sets a poor example for his subjects, who look to him for guidance
sentence_text_1: A rightful king prioritizes the good of the public over his own private ends
